figures.caption: FIG 3. Log-transformed mean squared errors for the BMLE and the IMLE with different σ values. The top left panel presents the estimation results for π. The bottom left panel is for µ1. The top right panel is for µ0. The bottom right panel is for Ω. The solid line with triangles represents the IMLE. The dashed line with circles represents the BMLE.
FIG 2. The illustration of the pipeline.

FIG 7. The illustration of a WSI sample used in the training data. The left panel (a) is the original image. The bright area in the middle panel (b) corresponds to the pixels with tissue information. The bright area in the right panel (c) corresponds to the pixels with metastatic carcinoma.

FIG 5. The boxplot of log(MSE) values for different estimators. The left panel illustrates the estimation results with different sample sizes. The right panel illustrates the estimation results for the SMLE with different pilot sample sizes.

FIG 1. The illustration of a WSI sample. The left panel is the origin 98,304× 82,432 WSI with a 2,048× 2,048 red bounding box. The right panel illustrates the zoomed-in sub-image of the bounded region in the left panel.
TABLE 1 Prediction results of different methods on the CAMELYON16 dataset. The top and second performances are highlighted in boldface and underline for each column.

FIG 4. Log-transformed mean squared errors of the IMLE, the BMLE, and the SMLE with different subsampling fractions. The top left panel presents the estimation results for π. The bottom left panel is for µ1. The top right is for µ0. The bottom right is for Ω. The dashed line represents the IMLE. The dotted line represents the BMLE. The solid line with circles represents the SMLE.

FIG 8. The illustration of a test WSI sample. The left panel is the ground truth label of tumors. The middle left panel is the predicted heatmap of tumors. The middle right panel is the sub-image with largest posterior probability of being a tumor. The right panel is the sub-image with the smallest posterior probability of being a tumor.
